commands.review_summary:
The PR adds Codex sandbox http/request host preflight, in-sandbox DNS and redirect checks with pinned addresses, proxy disabling for the Python helper, and focused blocked-target tests.

PR surface: Source +92, Tests +55. Total +147 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. for the review finding: source inspection plus a Python ipaddress check show the new sandbox classifier does not block 100.100.100.200 or fec0::1. I did not run the PR's Vitest command or an end-to-end sandbox request.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Protected maintainer/security-sensitive PR with a concrete P1 policy-parity finding; maintainer or security-owner review should decide the final guard before merge.

target.title: fix(codex): guard sandbox http requests
target.body:
## Summary

Harden the Codex sandbox exec-server HTTP bridge so sandbox `http/request` rejects private/internal HTTP targets before dispatch and keeps the in-sandbox request path bound to validated DNS results.

## Changes

- Add TypeScript-side URL scheme and host/IP preflight before buffered or streaming sandbox HTTP execution starts.
- Add in-sandbox Python checks for blocked hostnames, private/internal/special-use resolved addresses, and redirect targets.
- Disable environment proxy handling for the bridge and pin validated DNS answers for the actual socket connection to avoid validation/connect drift.
- Cover blocked private and metadata-style targets in the focused Codex sandbox HTTP tests.

## Validation

- `corepack pnpm install --frozen-lockfile`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/codex/src/app-server/sandbox-exec-server.http.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local` found one DNS pinning issue; fixed it.
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/codex/src/app-server/sandbox-exec-server.http.test.ts`
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local` clean, no accepted/actionable findings.

## Notes

AI-assisted security hardening PR by @eleqtrizit. The related private tracking context is intentionally not expanded in this public PR body.
target.author: eleqtrizit
